# How to handle a multipart/form-data file upload with a typed struct param.
#
# The receiver method takes a struct whose fields are bound from the parsed
# multipart form. Text fields use the standard form-binding rules; file
# fields must be *multipart.FileHeader or []*multipart.FileHeader.

muxt generate --use-receiver-type=T
muxt check

exec go test -cover

-- template.gohtml --
{{define "POST /upload 201 Upload(multipart)" }}<p>uploaded {{.Result}}</p>{{end}}

-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.22

-- template.go --
package server

import (
	"embed"
	"html/template"
	"io"
	"mime/multipart"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type UploadForm struct {
	Caption string                `name:"caption"`
	File    *multipart.FileHeader `name:"file"`
}

type T struct{}

// Upload reads the uploaded file into memory and returns its byte length.
func (T) Upload(form UploadForm) int {
	if form.File == nil {
		return 0
	}
	f, err := form.File.Open()
	if err != nil {
		return 0
	}
	defer f.Close()
	body, _ := io.ReadAll(f)
	return len(body)
}

-- template_test.go --
package server

import (
	"bytes"
	"mime/multipart"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"testing"
)

func Test(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, T{})

	body := &bytes.Buffer{}
	w := multipart.NewWriter(body)
	if err := w.WriteField("caption", "my upload"); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	fw, err := w.CreateFormFile("file", "doc.txt")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if _, err := fw.Write([]byte("hello world")); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if err := w.Close(); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/upload", body)
	req.Header.Set("content-type", w.FormDataContentType())
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()

	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
	if rec.Result().StatusCode != http.StatusCreated {
		t.Errorf("status = %d, want %d", rec.Result().StatusCode, http.StatusCreated)
	}
}
